UOM WHEN TO USE 1: Powder-form drugs in a vial that must be reconstituted before administration
UOM WHEN TO USE 2: Liquid-form drugs supplied in a vial or pre-filled syringe
UOM WHEN TO USE 3: Ointments, creams, inhalers, or bulk powders dispensed by weight
UOM BILLING UNIT 3: Actual grams dispensed — primarily retail pharmacy
UOM WHEN TO USE 4: Factor VIII Antihemophilic Factor and similar biologics billed in international units
Key Rule: The UOM must match the physical form of the drug as supplied on the date of service — not how you prefer to bill it, and not what a prior claim used.
